project_reference.core_research_question: Can the existing q14/q80 perturbed_echo pipeline be turned into a fixed-observable OLE workflow that yields a decisive q14 small-delta benchmark and a credible hardware-ready path without overclaiming the meaning of q80 subset observables?

scope.question: Can the existing q14/q80 perturbed_echo workflow be extended into a fixed-observable OLE path that starts with a q14 small-delta F_delta(P) vs delta^2 benchmark, with explicit normalized translation to f_delta(O), against the current perturbed_echo baseline and stays hardware-ready while keeping both q80 subset and possible full-q80 directions in view?
in_scope: Fixed-observable OLE built from the existing q14/q80 perturbed_echo workflow
q14 small-delta OLE-vs-delta^2 benchmark against the current perturbed_echo baseline as the first success gate
Classical exact/reference and hardware-ready measurement paths
Both overlap-support and disjoint-support variants between the fixed observable and perturbation generator
q80 subset observables as the immediate scalable route
Full-q80 OLE kept visible as a later extension rather than dropped
out_of_scope: Restoring the legacy full Level-C two-size claim during initial project setup
Presenting q80 subset results as if they already settle full-q80 global OLE
Relabeling existing perturbed_echo outputs as OLE without implementing the fixed-observable protocol
unresolved_questions: Which fixed observable placement should be the default first implementation for the q14 benchmark?
Whether full-q80 OLE will admit a feasible meaningful proxy or remain a longer-horizon target
Whether later phases should use explicit PLE terminology for a Pauli-specialized fixed-observable OLE path

user_asserted_anchors: Stay centered on the existing q14-q80 workflow
Both classical and hardware-ready paths must remain in scope
Use fixed observables rather than an observable-family search
Keep q80 as subset or as full in sight
known_good_baselines: Current perturbed_echo baseline in the q14-only exact-short campaign
Subset-observable locality evidence in the q80/level-B artifact trail
context_gaps: A stronger external benchmark beyond the Algorithmiq OLE theory note has not yet been selected
crucial_inputs: First success signal: a q14 small-delta F_delta(P)-vs-delta^2 benchmark with explicit f_delta(O) translation against the current perturbed_echo baseline
Reject false progress from a classical-only curve with no hardware-ready protocol
Carry both overlap-support and disjoint-support variants forward

formulations: Fixed-observable operator Loschmidt echo f_delta(O) with small-delta expansion connected to the OTOC/commutator diagnostic
Bridge from existing state-echo perturbed_echo machinery to measurement protocols for fixed O = Z_Q or related fixed observables
allowed_estimator_families: Exact classical estimator for q14 small-delta OLE curves
Hardware-ready parity-correlator or equivalent fixed-observable measurement protocols consistent with the Algorithmiq OLE note
Subset-observable estimators for q80 as an explicitly scoped partial observable
forbidden_estimator_families: Proxy-only reuse of perturbed_echo with no fixed-observable OLE implementation
Implicitly treating subset-only q80 observables as full-system global OLE
allowed_fit_families: Near-zero-delta fits in delta^2 motivated by the small-delta OLE expansion
Direct baseline overlays against current perturbed_echo outputs where the observable definitions remain explicit
forbidden_fit_families: Convenience fits that hide whether the result is truly OLE or only a renamed perturbed_echo proxy
stop_and_rethink_conditions: The first benchmark only reproduces a qualitative trend and cannot be tied to an explicit fixed-observable OLE definition
The hardware-ready protocol collapses back to a classical-only state-overlap proxy rather than a fixed-observable measurement path
A q80 subset result is being interpreted as if the full-q80 global question were already answered

observables.name: q14 fixed-observable OLE curve
observables.definition: The q14 small-delta fixed-observable operator Loschmidt echo reported first as F_delta(P) plotted against delta^2 for the chosen Pauli observable and perturbation generator, with explicit normalized translation to f_delta(O).

observables.name: q14 perturbed_echo baseline
observables.definition: The existing q14 perturbed_echo baseline from the current exact-short workflow used as the immediate comparison anchor for the first OLE benchmark.

observables.name: q80 OLE scope path
observables.definition: The explicit distinction between immediate q80 subset-observable OLE work and the longer-horizon full-q80 global OLE direction.

claims.statement: The project's first decisive result is a q14 small-delta fixed-observable OLE benchmark that can be compared directly against the current perturbed_echo baseline while preserving a hardware-ready path.
The project must keep q80 subset observables as the immediate scalable route while keeping the full-q80 OLE direction explicit rather than silently collapsing scope to subsets only.

deliverables.description: Report or figure overlaying the q14 small-delta fixed-observable OLE curve with the current q14 perturbed_echo baseline.
must_contain: baseline overlay to current perturbed_echo output

deliverables.description: A hardware-ready note describing how the fixed-observable OLE measurement path maps onto the existing pipeline.
A note that separates immediate q80 subset-observable OLE work from longer-horizon full-q80 global OLE goals.
must_contain: no overclaiming of subset as full-system result

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-q14-ole-bridge
acceptance_tests.procedure: Produce the q14 small-delta fixed-observable OLE-vs-delta^2 benchmark and compare it against the current perturbed_echo baseline with the observable definitions stated explicitly.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: A q14 benchmark artifact exists with the OLE curve, the perturbed_echo baseline overlay, and explicit fixed-observable definitions.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-q14-ole-bridge
acceptance_tests.procedure: Show that the first-step OLE framing includes an explicit hardware-ready measurement path rather than stopping at a classical-only construction.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: A hardware-ready note exists that maps the fixed-observable OLE protocol onto the existing measurement pipeline and names its current limitations.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-q80-visibility
acceptance_tests.procedure: Review project artifacts to confirm that q80 subset observables are presented as the immediate route while full-q80 global OLE remains an explicit unresolved extension.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Project artifacts preserve both q80 subset and full-q80 directions without presenting subset-only results as settling the full-q80 question.

references.locator: Algorithmiq, 'Model Description and Theory: Information flow in complex materials' (PDF, accessed 2026-03-17)
references.why_it_matters: Provides the fixed-observable OLE definition, its small-delta expansion, and the hardware-oriented measurement framing used to bridge from the existing pipeline.
Freezes the current narrow q14-only claim language and records the q12 blocker and q80 subset-only interpretation limits.
Defines the active q14 exact-short campaign settings that the first OLE benchmark must stay aligned with.
Carries forward the current q80 subset-observable evidence trail and its locality interpretation without overstating it as full-system validation.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-q14-ole-bridge
forbidden_proxies.proxy: A clean q14 classical-only curve with no hardware-ready protocol
forbidden_proxies.reason: Would look like progress while dropping the hardware-ready requirement the user explicitly kept in scope.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-q14-ole-bridge
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Renaming or refitting existing perturbed_echo outputs without implementing fixed-observable OLE
forbidden_proxies.reason: Would hide the distinction between the current state-echo observable and the requested fixed-observable OLE path.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-q80-visibility
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Presenting q80 subset-observable results as if they already settle the full-q80 global OLE question
forbidden_proxies.reason: Would overclaim beyond the explicit scope boundary and blur the unresolved full-q80 extension.

weakest_anchors: The external benchmark layer is currently anchored mainly by the Algorithmiq theory note rather than a broader literature set
unvalidated_assumptions: The current perturbed_echo workflow can be cleanly bridged to a fixed-observable OLE implementation without hidden estimator mismatches
A hardware-ready fixed-observable measurement path can stay aligned with the existing q14/q80 pipeline constraints
competing_explanations: Apparent agreement in the small-delta regime could come from a renamed or closely related perturbed_echo proxy rather than a genuine fixed-observable OLE implementation
disconfirming_observations: The first q14 benchmark cannot be expressed as an explicit fixed-observable OLE curve against delta^2
Keeping q80 subset and full-q80 directions both visible proves operationally incoherent under the current pipeline

position.last_activity_desc: Phase 2 research, experiment design, and plan files created

open_questions: Whether full-q80 OLE will admit a feasible meaningful proxy or remain a longer-horizon target
How small the accepted q14 fit window must be before the overlap coefficient stabilizes numerically at nonzero scrambling depth
Whether later phases should use explicit PLE terminology for a Pauli-specialized fixed-observable OLE path

convention_lock.gauge_choice: not applicable (no gauge field in gate-model circuit benchmark)
convention_lock.regularization_scheme: not applicable (finite-dimensional Hilbert space; no UV regulator)
convention_lock.renormalization_scheme: not applicable (no renormalized continuum coupling in current scope)
convention_lock.spin_basis: computational basis with Pauli operators X,Y,Z in the standard qubit basis
convention_lock.coupling_convention: brickwork gate angles are used directly; no separate continuum coupling convention
convention_lock.index_positioning: qubit and operator labels are positional only; no covariant/contravariant distinction
convention_lock.time_ordering: circuit gates are ordered by layer depth from left to right in circuit time
convention_lock.levi_civita_sign: epsilon_xyz = +1 for Pauli-axis orientation when used
convention_lock.generator_normalization: single-qubit Pauli generators square to identity; use X_q, Y_q, Z_q without 1/2 factors unless stated
convention_lock.covariant_derivative_sign: not applicable (no covariant derivative in the circuit benchmark)
convention_lock.gamma_matrix_convention: not applicable (no gamma matrices in the circuit benchmark)
convention_lock.creation_annihilation_order: not applicable (no creation/annihilation ordering is used in the current scope)

custom_conventions.observable_normalization: Report the first q14 benchmark as F_delta(P) with F_0(P)=1 for Pauli P, and keep f_delta(O) = 2^-n F_delta(P) as the normalized translation with Tr(O^2)=1.
custom_conventions.operator_picture: Use the fixed bridge picture A_P(U)=U P U^dagger and rotate the generator as well when translating into an alternate U^dagger O U picture.
custom_conventions.qubit_indexing: Qubits are indexed 0..n-1 as in Qiskit; subset observables must report their support S explicitly.
custom_conventions.perturbed_echo_baseline: perturbed_echo denotes the state-return benchmark |<psi0|U^dagger X U|psi0>|^2 for psi0=|0...0>; it is a baseline and must not be labeled as OLE.
